page: Vultr vhf-1c-2gb is a virtual machine instance within the High Frequency Compute family. It features 1 shared Intel vCPU running at 2.0 GHz on the x86_64 architecture, with 1 physical core and 1 thread. The processor includes 32 KB of L1d cache, 4 MB of L2 cache, and 16 MB of L3 cache, supporting instruction sets such as AVX, AES, BMI2, and FMA. This instance is equipped with 2.0 GB of system memory and includes 64 GB of local NVMe SSD storage, alongside one complimentary public IPv4 address.

Benchmark results indicate entry-level performance characteristics. The single-core and multi-core stress-ng CPU tests both score 1266.88, reflecting its single-threaded shared CPU allocation. Memory bandwidth is limited, with large-block read and write speeds recorded at 11.09 GB/sec and 10.16 GB/sec respectively, while small-block cached reads reach 100.1 GB/sec. Single-threaded gzip compression performs at an average rate of 24.19 GB/sec, though bzip3 decompression is weaker at 8.48 GB/sec. Database and web serving capabilities are modest, with Redis SET operations handling 1.77 million operations per second and static web serving throughput reaching 1.68 GB/sec.

Given its shared CPU model and low resource footprint, this instance offers a highly cost-effective entry point for lightweight utility workloads. The inclusion of local NVMe storage provides fast disk access relative to the overall resource constraints, though the shared CPU allocation and limited memory bandwidth make it unsuitable for resource-intensive applications. It is best suited for basic web serving, lightweight background tasks, small-scale development environments, and simple single-threaded automation scripts.
description: Vultr vhf-1c-2gb is an entry-level virtual server from the High Frequency Compute family, featuring 1 shared Intel x86_64 vCPU clocked at 2.0 GHz, 2.0 GB of RAM, and 64 GB of local NVMe SSD storage. Built on a single-core, single-thread architecture with 16 MB of L3 cache, this instance supports AVX, AES, and FMA instruction sets. Performance benchmarks place this server in the entry-level tier, with a stress-ng CPU score of 1266.88 and a Passmark memory score of 980.81. While memory bandwidth and database operations are limited, the instance achieves an average single-threaded gzip compression speed of 24.19 GB/sec. This configuration represents a highly resource-dense, budget-friendly option for basic utility tasks. It is best suited for lightweight workloads such as static web serving, small development environments, simple automation scripts, and basic single-threaded applications.
og_description: Vultr vhf-1c-2gb is a budget-friendly virtual server featuring 1 shared Intel vCPU, 2.0 GB of RAM, and 64 GB of local NVMe SSD storage, designed for lightweight utility workloads and basic web serving.
